dsl_rule.translation_notes: Mean reversion strategy using one-day returns (GS) with ±1% and ±3% thresholds. Entry conditions trigger on crosses of these thresholds in either direction. Exit uses maximum holding period of 20 days (middle of specified range 1-60 days) combined with mean reversion exit logic where long positions exit on -3% drop and short positions exit on +3% rise. The 'returns' indicator represents one-day price change in decimal format (0.01 = 1%).

backtest_rule.reason: Mean reversion strategy using one-day returns (GS) with ±1% and ±3% thresholds. Entry conditions trigger on crosses of these thresholds in either direction. Exit uses maximum holding period of 20 days (middle of specified range 1-60 days) combined with mean reversion exit logic where long positions exit on -3% drop and short positions exit on +3% rise. The 'returns' indicator represents one-day price change in decimal format (0.01 = 1%).

source_rule_text: Mean reversion strategy that enters long or short positions when one-day returns of GS cross predefined percent-change thresholds, with exit after a specified holding period.
